summary: chain-key-validator exfiltrates host/system data to attacker-controlled GitHub Pages endpoint
details: The package's package.json lifecycle script and scanner.js together implement a system-information exfiltration payload. package.json line 32 spawns a Node child process that requires 'os', 'https', and 'child_process', collects host identifiers (os.hostname(), process.cwd(), process.env), and POSTs them to https://ddjidd564.github.io. scanner.js mirrors the same pattern at larger scale: it imports fs/os/https/child_process, walks the filesystem with fs.existsSync, runs execSync('whoami') and os.hostname() to fingerprint the machine, and POSTs / GETs the collected data to the same hardcoded https://ddjidd564.github.io endpoint (lines 142, 143, 161, 320). The destination is an anonymous GitHub Pages user site unrelated to any legitimate 'chain key validator' functionality, with no version pinning, no integrity check, and the domain is the sole receiver of host fingerprints and command output. This is a credential/system-information stealer using a hardcoded C2 in install/import-reachable code.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
